evidence: Both src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts and src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts execute deprecation-policy lookups at module load and export policy metadata, spreading governance concerns into runtime facades.
src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx hardcodes '/tools' and a Discord URL even though src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts is positioned as the shared source of tool routing constants.

src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts derives its filter type via Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3], creating a hidden dependency on another module’s positional signature instead of a stable shared boundary type.

`useContentResponsive.test.ts` encodes pane-aware expectations (`contentBreakpoints`, `usePanes` mock), while `useContentResponsive.ts` is viewport-only (`window.innerWidth/innerHeight`), signaling unclear ownership of 'content' vs 'viewport' responsibility.
The assigned `Slot.tsx` lives in `shared/lib` even though it is a UI composition primitive by behavior and API, which blurs architectural layer boundaries.

Assigned files indicate shared-root sprawl instead of domain-aligned ownership boundaries (clipboard behavior, responsive behavior, settings persistence, and low-level utilities all represented as root-level units).

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx introduces a large `useImageLightboxEnvironment` return object and threads it through multiple internal layers, increasing pass-through abstraction cost.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx builds large composite models (`lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, `layoutProps`) with very broad dependency lists, indicating abstraction layers are carrying too much mutable surface.
src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx then rehydrates many context slices and prop groups, so complexity is shifted across wrappers rather than reduced.
src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts operates against `queryKeys.unified`, `queryKeys.shots`, and `queryKeys.generations` simultaneously for one mutation path.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts additionally invalidates `unifiedGenerationQueryKeys.all` and `queryKeys.generations.all` in the same flow, showing overlapping cache-key ecosystems.
src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx invalidates via `unifiedGenerationQueryKeys.projectPrefix`, reinforcing split dependency usage for generation cache concerns.
src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts constructs orchestrator payloads with many shape variants and conditional keys at the producer boundary.
supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts must read expected segment count through multiple fallback locations (`orchestrator_details.*` then top-level), signaling boundary contract drift.
supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts performs route resolution from snapshot + raw params, adding another translation seam for the same payload family.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts mixes DB lookup/delete, cache mutation, query invalidation, operation-result adaptation, and legacy callback wrappers in one hook.
src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts composes many mode hooks and persistence wiring with large state/value surfaces, making local reasoning difficult.
Both files show dense control flow and adapter layering where extracting smaller focused units would improve directness.

Task parent/child detection is not coherent across modules: `useTaskStatusCounts` only excludes children via `params->orchestrator_task_id_ref` (src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:55-77), while billing/orchestrator logic supports additional legacy paths (`params->orchestrator_details->>orchestrator_task_id`, `params->>orchestrator_task_id`) in shared filters (supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:114-120).

Status semantics differ for the same domain concept: `useTaskStatusCounts` explicitly excludes `Cancelled` from failures (src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:69-75), but orchestrator completion treats `Cancelled` as failed segment work (supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:265-267, 551-553).

The generate-video request builder exposes a mixed v1/v2 surface with many optional backward-compat fields in one input (`TravelRequestBodyInput`) and silent defaults (`normalizeTravelRequestBodyInput`) rather than one strict contract (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:432-487).

src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts contains multiple thin wrappers (`getFramePositionForIndex`, `getAspectRatioStyle`) with long explanatory comments that mostly restate called utilities.

src/shared/lib/storagePaths.ts and src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts have high comment-to-logic density, including many repetitive section banners and explanatory blocks for straightforward type/constant declarations.
src/shared/lib/taskCreation/ids.ts documents simple built-in/fallback behavior in detail, mirroring obvious code flow.

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts keeps legacy fields in the main input contract by extending `LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` and allowing `LegacyStructureVideoConfig[]` on `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput`.

src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts accepts a broad mixed old+new parameter bag and still infers legacy mode from legacy path fields, so migration boundaries remain in active creation paths.
src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts and src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts show deprecated aliases still exported from live modules, keeping old import surfaces active.

Packet structure metrics show `src/shared/hooks/` is highly flat (`file_count: 85`) while assigned files remain in root-level hook paths (`useAutoSaveSettings.ts`, `useContentResponsive.ts`, `useCopyToClipboard.ts`) instead of stable capability subfolders.

Assigned tests `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts` still import via `../...`, reinforcing root-level placement even though these concerns are already subgroup-worthy (clipboard/responsive/settings).

Packet structure metrics show `src/shared/lib/` is also flat (`file_count: 73`) and assigned files mix unrelated concerns in one directory: UI composition primitive (`Slot.tsx`), cache container (`ProjectScopedCache.ts`), and key-transform utility (`caseConversion.ts`).

Coupling matrix confirms strong hooks→lib coupling (`src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/ = 50`) but current placement does not separate low-level utility, UI primitive, and feature-support responsibilities.

Task-construction modules repeat similar orchestration assembly patterns with separate ad-hoc builders (`buildJoinClipsPayload`, `buildIndividualTravelSegmentParams`, and `createImageGenerationTask` payload composition), creating parallel contract-writing paths.

`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` (656 lines) coordinates drag, zoom, selection, drop interception, endpoint drag, prefetch, and structure-video placement in one hook, while `useEditingController.ts` composes another wide state/action bundle for unrelated editing and join workflows.

Image generation submission flow tunnels a large prop/state surface through `useFormSubmission -> useSubmissionHandlers -> useSubmissionOrchestrator`, and by-reference validation/toast logic is duplicated in both `useSubmissionOrchestrator.ts` and `submissionTaskPlan.ts`.

Edge functions show inconsistent request pipeline architecture: some use runtime/bootstrap helpers (`initEdgeRuntime`, `jsonResponse`) while others manually implement env boot, method checks, auth, JSON parsing, and raw `Response` handling.

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` still exposes `getLegacySupabaseClient()` and explicitly documents a different bootstrap/auth path (`initializeSupabase()` migration warning), while active modules in this batch (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`) use `getSupabaseClient()` directly.
This leaves two client-access patterns alive for privileged data mutations/reads, which increases risk of inconsistent auth/session behavior across call sites until migration completes.
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Supabase client access is not yet fully converged on one auth/session entrypoint; legacy facade remains a live alternate path.

findings.summary: Deprecation-governance logic is embedded in multiple runtime facades at import time.
evidence: handleError.ts initializes runtimeErrorAliasPolicy at module scope and exports owner/remove-by/import-budget metadata.
legacySupabaseFacade.ts initializes legacySupabasePolicy at module scope and exports the same style of governance metadata plus module-local warning state.
This repeats a governance pattern in unrelated runtime modules, increasing coupling and making import behavior depend on policy plumbing.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecation-policy metadata and budgeting into a centralized governance registry module, and keep facades as thin runtime adapters that import a precomputed contract object. This removes repeated module-scope policy initialization and narrows each facade’s responsibility.

findings.summary: Home pane bypasses shared route constants with hardcoded navigation strings.
evidence: CreativePartnerPane calls navigate('/tools') directly instead of using shared route constants.
toolConstants.ts documents itself as the single source of truth for tool identifiers/routes, but this pane bypasses that boundary.
When route contracts change, this path can drift independently from the shared constants module.
findings.suggestion: Introduce/extend shared route constants for generic tool entry points (e.g., TOOLS_HOME) and replace hardcoded navigation strings in pane components with those constants to enforce one boundary for routing contracts.

findings.summary: Media-gallery item behavior is split across wide state bags and UI components with workflow logic.
evidence: useMediaGalleryItemState returns a broad mutable surface (multiple local states and setters), creating a large coupling surface for consumers.
ShotActions receives four prop groups yet still decides workflow behavior (navigate vs add, quick-create flow), so business orchestration lives inside a presentational component.
The split suggests missing higher-level controller abstraction for shot-action workflow ownership.
findings.suggestion: Create a dedicated shot-action controller hook that owns workflow transitions and exposes a smaller intent-level API to ShotActions (e.g., primaryAction, secondaryAction, selectedShotModel). Keep ShotActions presentational and reduce direct setter/state bag exposure from useMediaGalleryItemState.

findings.summary: Lightbox abstraction layers pass oversized state models instead of reducing complexity.
evidence: `useImageLightboxEnvironment` and render-model hooks aggregate many unrelated concerns (layout, fetching, edit mode, variant actions) into large pass-through objects.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` has wide memo/dependency surfaces, indicating high indirection upkeep cost.
Layout consumes these broad contracts through multiple context hooks, so abstraction boundaries remain leaky.
findings.suggestion: Split lightbox contracts into narrow, stable slices (for example: `mediaDisplayModel`, `variantActions`, `panelModel`, `navigationModel`) and have `LightboxLayout` consume only the slices it renders. Keep edit-mode specifics in dedicated providers so non-edit rendering paths avoid carrying those fields.

findings.summary: Generation cache invalidation depends on overlapping key systems, increasing coupling and stale-data risk.
evidence: Mutation and delete flows invalidate both `queryKeys.*` and `unifiedGenerationQueryKeys.*` paths for the same domain data.
Multiple broad invalidations are needed to maintain consistency, indicating no single canonical dependency surface for generation caches.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical generation-query key contract and add a temporary compatibility adapter for legacy callers. Migrate mutation hooks and feature pages to that contract, then remove duplicate invalidation calls.

findings.summary: Producer and consumer seams for orchestrator payloads rely on fallback parsing instead of one explicit contract.
evidence: Payload builder emits complex orchestrator-details structures with many optional/conditional keys.
Orchestrator completion resolves key values from multiple potential locations (`orchestrator_details` and root-level fallbacks).
Generation routing separately reconstructs semantics from snapshot + raw params, creating parallel translation paths.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared typed contract module for orchestration payloads with encode/decode functions used by both client task builders and Supabase complete-task handlers. Reject malformed contracts at decode boundaries rather than applying multi-path fallbacks.

findings.summary: Large hooks combine persistence, mutation, adapters, and UI wiring in single bodies.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` includes direct Supabase CRUD, query-cache edits, invalidation policy, and legacy adapter wrapping in one hook.
`useInlineEditState` centralizes environment setup, persistence syncing, edit-mode orchestration, and state-shape assembly into a single local subsystem.
findings.suggestion: Refactor into layered internals: (1) repository/mutation utilities, (2) cache policy helpers, (3) UI-facing adapter hooks. Keep each hook focused on one responsibility and return minimal state/action contracts.

findings.summary: Task-count API uses only canonical child-task path, but sibling modules still support legacy child references.
evidence: `useTaskStatusCounts` excludes children only with `.is('params->orchestrator_task_id_ref', null)` (src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:55-77).

Shared billing/orchestrator lookup still queries legacy orchestrator reference fields (`params->orchestrator_details->>orchestrator_task_id`, `params->originalParams...`, `params->>orchestrator_task_id`) (supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:114-120).
This means legacy child tasks can leak into status-count aggregates as if they were parent tasks, producing inconsistent API behavior between list/count/completion subsystems.
findings.suggestion: Centralize parent-task filtering in one shared helper (reused by React query hooks and edge functions) that includes canonical plus legacy reference paths until migration is complete, then remove legacy paths in one coordinated change.

findings.summary: Cancelled tasks are treated as failures in orchestrator completion but not in task-status metrics.
evidence: Task-status counts intentionally exclude `Cancelled` from failures (`eq('status', 'Failed')` and comment documenting that cancellation is user-initiated) in src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:69-75.

Orchestrator segment summary counts both `Failed` and `Cancelled` as failed segments in supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:265-267 and maps final-step `Cancelled` to `'failed'` in :551-553.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared task-status policy for orchestration and UI metrics (including explicit `Cancelled` semantics) and apply it in both modules so user-visible counts and orchestrator state transitions reflect the same contract.

findings.summary: Generate-video request builder exposes an overloaded mixed-version input that silently fabricates defaults.
evidence: `TravelRequestBodyInput` accepts both v2 grouped fields and many backward-compat flat fields in one type (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:432-452).

`normalizeTravelRequestBodyInput` silently injects defaults for missing legacy fields (e.g., amountOfMotion=50, motionMode='basic', seed=0) rather than enforcing explicit caller intent (same file:454-487).

A similar compat adapter pattern exists in join clips (`JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` -> `toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput`), indicating repeated mixed-shape API surfaces instead of strict boundary conversion ownership (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts:24-254).
findings.suggestion: Split public APIs into strict versioned entry points (`buildTravelRequestBodyV2` only for callers) and keep compatibility translation private at one ingress layer; reject ambiguous mixed-shape input instead of auto-defaulting omitted fields.

findings.summary: Cross-module seam normalization is fragmented, creating inconsistent fail-open/fail-closed behavior.
evidence: Shared payload normalizer marks invalid numeric candidates explicitly (`invalid` flag) in supabase/functions/_shared/payloadNormalization.ts:22-44.
Task-details normalization performs ad-hoc coercion to empty records without carrying invalid-state metadata in src/shared/components/TaskDetails/hooks/normalizeTaskDetailsPayload.ts:11-31.
Edge request parsing has both strict and loose modes and also a legacy response adapter (`OperationResult` vs `{ body?, errorResponse? }`) in supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:140-223.
Billing has separate JSON record parsing logic (`parseJsonRecord`) with yet another null-on-invalid contract in supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:221-230.
findings.suggestion: Create a single boundary-normalization module for object/string/int parsing and one edge-handler result contract; migrate call sites to it so invalid payloads are classified consistently and orchestration logic no longer needs per-module adapter glue.

findings.summary: Canonical task input types still embed legacy fields, extending dual-path migration debt.
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` extends `LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` and allows `LegacyStructureVideoConfig[]`, so deprecated fields remain first-class in the main read/write input surface.
Join-clips adapter accepts both new (`clip_source`, `video_edit`) and old (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`) fields in one interface, then normalizes later.
findings.suggestion: Narrow legacy acceptance to dedicated boundary adapters only: keep canonical input contracts legacy-free, add explicit legacy parser modules that normalize to canonical DTOs before entering shared task creation paths, and remove legacy fields from primary exported input types.

findings.summary: Deprecated aliases are governed inconsistently across modules, risking indefinite legacy retention.
evidence: legacySupabaseFacade and legacyStructureVideo are tied to deprecationPolicy metadata/import budgets.
handleError still exports deprecated alias symbols (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) but does not bind them to deprecationPolicy budget/removal gating in the same way.
findings.suggestion: Apply one deprecation lifecycle pattern to all legacy aliases: attach policy metadata and budget checks, emit policy-driven warnings, and enforce removal through a single governance helper so all deprecated exports follow the same sunset mechanism.

findings.summary: Several helper modules use verbose ceremony and thin wrappers that add little behavioral value.
evidence: image-utils includes wrappers that mostly forward to imported helpers with extensive explanatory comments.
storagePaths and taskCreation/ids document straightforward operations in long blocks, increasing scan cost without clarifying non-obvious domain constraints.
findings.suggestion: Trim restating comments and collapse no-op wrappers unless they enforce policy/validation; keep comments only where they capture domain invariants or non-obvious constraints.

findings.summary: Task type classification uses implicit substring fallback, weakening precise control flow.
evidence: `isTravelTaskType` returns true for `taskType.includes('travel')` in addition to explicit set membership.
This undermines the module’s stated single-source-of-truth approach and can classify unforeseen strings as travel types.
findings.suggestion: Remove substring fallback and rely on explicit registries sourced from one canonical task-type map; if extensibility is needed, add explicit prefix/namespace rules in that map rather than inline string heuristics.

findings.summary: Hook files stay in shared root while behavior-specific ownership is split and hard to navigate
evidence: All three assigned production hooks are placed directly under `src/shared/hooks/`, despite representing distinct capabilities (clipboard, responsive layout, settings persistence).
`src/shared/hooks/` has very high flatness (`file_count: 85` from packet structure), making root-level placement an active navigation burden.
The assigned copy hook test imports `../useCopyToClipboard`, which preserves root placement instead of converging on a single capability folder.
findings.suggestion: Stage the reorg in one pass: (1) create target folders `src/shared/hooks/clipboard/`, `src/shared/hooks/responsive/`, `src/shared/hooks/settings/`; (2) move files in order `useCopyToClipboard.ts`, `useContentResponsive.ts`, `useAutoSaveSettings.ts`; (3) update imports and test paths (including assigned tests) with `rg -n "useCopyToClipboard|useContentResponsive|useAutoSaveSettings" src`; (4) run `pnpm test src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit` to validate; (5) if temporary compatibility is needed, keep one-cycle re-export stubs then remove.

findings.summary: Content-responsive naming and tests no longer match viewport-only implementation ownership
evidence: Implementation is purely viewport-based (`window.innerWidth`, resize listener), but test suite is structured around pane-context `contentBreakpoints` semantics.
This mismatch indicates architectural drift: the module name and boundary imply content-area ownership while implementation owns viewport behavior.
findings.suggestion: Use a staged boundary cleanup: (1) choose target folder and contract (`src/shared/hooks/responsive/useViewportResponsive.ts` for viewport behavior, or a true content-aware hook under pane/layout ownership); (2) move/rename implementation first; (3) update assigned tests to match selected contract and import path; (4) run `pnpm test src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts` (or renamed test) and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`; (5) remove old alias exports after all imports are migrated.

findings.summary: UI primitive, cache class, and key-conversion utility are co-located in one generic lib root
evidence: `Slot.tsx` is a React UI composition primitive but is placed in `shared/lib/` rather than a UI/component boundary.
`ProjectScopedCache.ts` (state/cache concern) and `caseConversion.ts` (string/key transform concern) are also in the same root, indicating concern mixing.
Packet structure confirms `src/shared/lib/` is broad and flat (`file_count: 73`), and these assigned files reinforce that mixed-concern pattern.
findings.suggestion: Apply a staged lib decomposition: (1) create `src/shared/components/ui/` (or `src/shared/ui/primitives/`) target for `Slot.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/cache/` for `ProjectScopedCache.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/utils/` (or `src/shared/lib/formatting/`) for `caseConversion.ts`; (2) move in that order to minimize React/UI import churn first; (3) update imports with `rg -n "@/shared/lib/(Slot|ProjectScopedCache|caseConversion)" src`; (4) run `pnpm test src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCredits.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`; (5) delete transient re-export shims after callers are migrated.

findings.summary: Deprecated Supabase facade remains a broad runtime dependency instead of a shrinking compatibility seam
evidence: `getLegacySupabaseClient()` is still exported as active runtime API and only warns after removal date (`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` lines 27-35, 50-53), so removal policy is advisory at runtime.

README still presents `@/integrations/supabase/legacy` as compatibility import location (`README.md` line 32), and holistic coupling shows 36 imports from `src/shared/hooks/` into legacy, indicating widespread cross-layer reliance.
findings.suggestion: Enforce the contract as a real boundary: keep one temporary migration adapter package (for example `src/integrations/supabase/migration/`), block new imports to `legacy/` in CI, and switch `getLegacySupabaseClient()` to hard-throw past target date in non-test builds. Then migrate highest-fan-in callers first (`src/shared/hooks/`), followed by UI-level callers, and finally remove the legacy entrypoint.

findings.summary: Error runtime facade is overloaded with governance metadata and compatibility aliases
evidence: Core runtime behavior (`handleError`) coexists with deprecation-policy exports (`RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_*`) and alias surface (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) in the same file (`handleError.ts` lines 13-19, 35-45, 57-63).
README’s deprecation section references this same file as contract locus (`README.md` lines 41-47), making a high-import runtime utility also a governance manifest.
findings.suggestion: Split concerns: keep `handleError.ts` as runtime-only API (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow`, `HandleErrorOptions`) and move alias lifecycle metadata/owner/remove-by exports into a governance contract module consumed by CI checks. Keep deprecated aliases in a dedicated compat file to isolate eventual deletion.

findings.summary: Canonical generation/shot type barrel stays in flat root types package despite domain-specific type subtree
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` defines canonical surface but re-exports from `@/types/*` root files (`generationAndShots.ts` lines 2-11).

README codifies that canonical path (`README.md` line 52), while structure data shows `src/types/` is a 12-file mixed-concern directory and a separate `src/domains/generation/types/` subtree exists, indicating package role overlap.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create `src/domains/generation/contracts/` as the single public type surface. (2) Move generation/shot contracts from `src/types/` into that folder in this order: `generationParams`, `generationMetadata`, `generationRow`, `shot`. (3) Convert `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` into a temporary compatibility barrel that re-exports from the new domain contracts and add deprecation tags. (4) Update imports in waves: first `src/tools/travel-between-images/**`, then `src/shared/hooks/**`, then remaining consumers. (5) Validate with `rg "@/types/(generation|shot|generationAndShots)" src`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run check:contracts`; remove the compatibility barrel once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Multiple task families reimplement orchestration payload assembly, increasing contract drift risk.
evidence: `joinClips.ts` builds a large `orchestratorDetails` object and then composes family payload (`buildJoinClipsPayload`, around lines 324+).
`individualTravelSegment.ts` separately rebuilds orchestration + task-view + family contract paths (`buildIndividualTravelSegmentParams`, around lines 555+).

`imageGeneration.ts` has a third payload path via `composeTaskParams` with custom segments/mapped fields in `createImageGenerationTask` (around lines 359+), rather than a single canonical contract builder.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one shared task-family payload composer with declarative field maps/validators per family, and keep per-task files focused on family-specific rules only.

findings.summary: Form submission hooks rely on long prop tunneling and duplicate guard logic across layers.
evidence: `useFormSubmission.ts` constructs and forwards a very large argument set (project, prompt, reference, hi-res, model, state refs) to downstream hooks.
`useSubmissionHandlers.ts` mostly forwards the same surface again into `useSubmissionOrchestrator`, indicating indirection without boundary reduction.
By-reference validation/toast (`Please upload a style reference image...`) is implemented in `useSubmissionOrchestrator.ts` and again in `submissionTaskPlan.ts`, so business rule ownership is split.
findings.suggestion: Create a single submission domain object (context + validators + command factory) and centralize validation rules so UI commands call one policy source.

findings.summary: Timeline/editing orchestration hooks aggregate too many responsibilities into broad control hubs.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` defines a very broad return contract including drag, zoom, selection, pending/drop state, endpoint editing, video browser handling, and computed pair data.
The hook performs orchestration for many independent concerns (drop interceptors, duplicate placement logic, zoom-center behavior, global event binding, structure-video placement) in one unit.
`useEditingController.ts` similarly merges structure video management, audio management, naming, and join workflow setup/handlers, then returns two large bundles (`mediaEditing`, `joinWorkflow`).
findings.suggestion: Split into smaller orchestration slices by concern (timeline interaction core, media-source insertion, selection/move workflow, join workflow) with a thin top-level composer.

findings.summary: Edge functions in the same subsystem use conflicting request/auth/response patterns instead of one coherent pipeline.
evidence: `generate-pat` uses `initEdgeRuntime` + `jsonResponse` and handles CORS preflight explicitly.

`generate-upload-url` and `update-shot-pair-prompts` manually load env vars, create clients, parse request bodies differently (`req.json()` vs `req.text()+JSON.parse`), and return raw `Response` with varied error formatting.
Auth/logging/method-check flow is conceptually similar but implemented separately in each file.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a shared edge-function request pipeline helper (method/cors/auth/body parsing/error mapping/logger binding) and migrate endpoints incrementally to the same contract.

findings.summary: Image lightbox remains a multi-role component with high coordination surface despite internal helper extraction.
evidence: `ImageLightbox.tsx` is 884 lines and combines environment setup, shared state, editing orchestration, controls panel assembly, render model construction, download/delete actions, and workflow wiring.
The file builds large callback bundles (`workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, `layoutProps`) with many optional handlers, indicating multiple concerns crossing a single boundary.
`VideoLightbox.tsx` is substantially smaller (206 lines), showing the image path has accumulated broader responsibilities than sibling lightbox modes.
findings.suggestion: Move render-model assembly and edit/workflow action wiring into dedicated modules, leaving `ImageLightbox` as a thin composition shell with stricter prop contracts.

findings.summary: Legacy and canonical Supabase client entrypoints coexist for authenticated data paths
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` exports `getLegacySupabaseClient()` and warns to migrate to `getSupabaseClient()` with bootstrap initialization, confirming a second auth/session access pattern is still live.

`useTasks.ts` and `useGenerationMutations.ts` perform task/generation queries and mutations via `getSupabaseClient()`; keeping both entrypoints increases chance that remaining legacy consumers run with different auth/session initialization semantics.
findings.suggestion: Finish convergence to a single client auth boundary: migrate remaining imports off `@/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade`, enforce via lint/import-ban rule, and remove the legacy facade once import count reaches zero.
